title: Isolation of Symbiodiniaceae from Coral Eggs Using Centrifugation
author: Sophie SCR Centurino Renton
materials: Isolate coral colonies at signs of setting.
If the required volume (calculated in step 11.

steps.title: Collect gamete bundles (hermaphroditic corals only)...
steps.instruction: Collect gamete bundles (hermaphroditic corals only) as they rise to the surface using cups and pipettes. N.B Label all collections with coral ID and time of spawning.

steps.title: Separate and wash gamete bundles (hermaphrodites) as per steps 3
steps.instruction: Separate and wash gamete bundles (hermaphrodites) as per steps 3.1 to 3.4. Wash station equipment for one genotype: 2 x bowls Access to running seawater Transfer pipettes 1 x filter mesh must be finer than the egg size of target species 10ml or 15ml falcon tubes

steps.title: Fill both bowls with seawater and...
steps.instruction: Fill both bowls with seawater and place the filter inside the first bowl. N.B. Ensure the mesh side of the filter is submerged.

steps.title: Pour the collected bundles into the...
steps.instruction: Pour the collected bundles into the filter and agitate the filter gently to break up the bundles. N.B. Eggs will remain inside the filter and the sperm will enter the bowl turning the water cloudy. Figure 1. Montipora aequituberculata gamete bundles inside 60um filter, submerged in seawater.

steps.title: Pick up the filter containing the...
steps.instruction: Pick up the filter containing the eggs and move it into the second bowl of seawater, submerging the eggs once again. N.B. Use the running seawater to wash down any eggs that get stuck on the inside of the filter. As sperm is not needed for fertilisation in this protocol, ensure thorough disposal of the (sperm) water before refreshing the bowls with seawater.

steps.title: Continue to gently agitate and transfer...
steps.instruction: Continue to gently agitate and transfer the gametes between bowls, refreshing the seawater, until all bundles are broken up and the water is no longer cloudy. N.B. Once all bundles are broken the individual eggs will form a mono-film on the surface of the water. Sperm cells interfere with the differential separation during centrifugation and must be completely removed in this step. Figure 2. Montipora aequituberculata eggs washed and separated from sperm.

steps.title: Utilise the positive buoyancy of the...
steps.instruction: Utilise the positive buoyancy of the eggs to transfer desired amounts into falcon tubes. Recommended egg-to-seawater ratios (see Figure 4 and Figure 5 for clarification): 15ml falcon tubes: 0.8ml eggs to 13ml seawater 10ml falcon tubes: 0.5ml eggs to 8ml seawater Figure

steps.title: Correctly filled falcon tubes (15ml) containing M
steps.instruction: Correctly filled falcon tubes (15ml) containing M.aequituberculata eggs packed with Symbiodiniaceae (positively buoyant) and seawater. Figure

steps.title: Incorrectly filled falcon tubes (15ml) containing...
steps.instruction: Incorrectly filled falcon tubes (15ml) containing too many eggs and too little seawater resulting in poor differential separation. N.B. Do not overload falcon tubes with eggs. Excessive numbers of eggs can result in the production of lipids from the death of the gametes. The lipids interfere with successful centrifugation.

steps.title: Centrifuge all falcon tubes in a...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all falcon tubes in a benchtop centrifuge at the below specifications: %s %s N.B. The first spin should have the most force. If there is limited access to high power centrifuge, use a low-power centrifuge at the maximum speed possible. The centrifugal force will break the egg walls and pellet the Symbiodiniaceae cells to the bottom of the falcon tube.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant by disposing of...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant by disposing of the seawater and the surface lipid layer (see Figure 7) into a liquid waste container. Tip: Firmly tap the falcon tube on the side of a liquid waste container to dislodge the lipid "pancake". Do this in one swift motion to dispose of both the lipid "pancake" and the seawater at the same time. The Symbiodiniaceae cell pellet will remain at the bottom of the falcon tube. Figure 7. The surface lipid layer ("lipid pancake") is shown in the black box

steps.title: Optional step
steps.instruction: Optional step: Combine samples (of the same genotype) to minimise the number of tubes to handle in the next round. N.B Do not combine separated cells with tubes that still contain lipids. No further centrifugation is needed once cells are isolated and the lipids have been removed.

steps.title: For samples that require another round...
steps.instruction: For samples that require another round of separation, top up all tubes with seawater to equal volumes of 10mL and pipette mix (3 times).

steps.title: Once no lipids remain and all...
steps.instruction: Once no lipids remain and all samples are cleaned of lipids and resemble figure 10, continue to

steps.title: N
steps.instruction: N.B There are different centrifuge specifications for rounds 2 and 3, see figures 11 and

steps.title: Record any deviations from these specifications...
steps.instruction: Record any deviations from these specifications and only conduct these rounds if necessary to remove lipids. A B 1 RCF 3000 x g 2 TIME 20 minutes 3 TEMP 23 degrees celsius Figure

steps.title: Centrifuge specifications for round
steps.instruction: Centrifuge specifications for round

steps.title: A B 1 RCF 2500 x...
steps.instruction: A B 1 RCF 2500 x g 2 TIME 12 minutes 3 TEMP 23 degrees Figure

steps.title: Centrifuge specifications for round 3
steps.instruction: Centrifuge specifications for round 3.

steps.title: with either of these techniques
steps.instruction: with either of these techniques: Load a 1ml pipette with seawater and rest the tip of the pipette on the inside wall of the falcon tube just above the pellet. Expel the seawater from the pipette using a steady force. The stream of seawater will lift the contact lipids from the pellet in one piece. N.B. The contact lipid layer is flaky and, in the best case, will lift in one piece. However, if the contact lipid comes off in multiple flakes, take the opportunity to remove each flake before trying to dislodge more.

steps.title: Gently scrape away the lipid layer...
steps.instruction: Gently scrape away the lipid layer with a pipette and disposable tips.

steps.title: If there are no or very...
steps.instruction: If there are no or very little contact lipids, continue directly to

steps.title: Contact lipids are those which are...
steps.instruction: Contact lipids are those which are in direct contact with the Symbiodiniaceae pellet. Careful removal of this layer is required. See the result of successful removal in figure

steps.title: Successful removal of the contact lipid...
steps.instruction: Successful removal of the contact lipid layer resulting in isolated Symbiodiniaceae cells.

steps.title: Once the lipids are removed, resuspend...
steps.instruction: Once the lipids are removed, resuspend the pellet in 1ml of seawater by pipette mixing with a 1000ul pipette. N.B pipette mix until no clumps remain, and the entire pellet is resuspended. Figure 10. Falcon tubes at the end of step 7 containing resuspended Symbiodiniaceae cells in 1 ml of seawater.

steps.title: According to their respective genotypes, combine...
steps.instruction: According to their respective genotypes, combine falcon tubes into a 250 mL flask, do not mix or contaminate genotypes. Label the flask with the appropriate genotypic identification. N.B. The flask containing the sample is called "master culture genotype x". If two genotypes, A and B, are being processed in this protocol, they will be separate and labelled master culture genotype A and master culture genotype B.

steps.title: exceeds the volume of the final...
steps.instruction: exceeds the volume of the final tubes (in this case 1.5mL Eppendorf tubes), follow steps 12.1, 12.2, 12.3 and proceed to step 12.

steps.title: is less than the volume of...
steps.instruction: is less than the volume of the final tubes (in this case 1.5mL Eppendorf tubes), go directly to step 12.4 and proceed to step 12.5.

steps.title: Total cell count calculations (steps 11
steps.instruction: Total cell count calculations (steps 11.2 and 11.3) (

steps.title: Dilution of Symbiodiniaceae cell abundance per...
steps.instruction: Dilution of Symbiodiniaceae cell abundance per millilitre of seawater to a workable density Figure

steps.title: Colour chart for cell density dilutions,...
steps.instruction: Colour chart for cell density dilutions, the sample in the black circle shows the colour for the correct dilution.

steps.title: Determine the number of cells per...
steps.instruction: Determine the number of cells per 1mL , this number is deemed "the Calculated Cell Count" (CCC). Tip: Use a haemocytometer that uses only 10ul of the sample. Given the microscopic nature of the Symbiodiniaceae cells, 10ul of a correctly diluted sample will have approximately 60 cells per grid. Larger quantities are likely to contain a much greater number of Symbiodiniaceae cells and large counts become unreliable. N.B. Homogenise the sample thoroughly each time before sub-sampling. Achieve this by pipette mixing 10 times around the flask. Example: CCC = 587,500 cells / 1mL

steps.title: Calculate the required volume for the Desired Cell Count (DCC)
steps.instruction: Calculate the required volume for the Desired Cell Count (DCC) . Example: The desired cell count is how many cells a given DNA extraction protocol requires to not overload the chemistries, for this example, the DNA extraction protocol requires 5 million cells. Therefore: DCC = 5,000,000 cells CCC = 587,500 cells/mL To calculate the required volume, divide the desired cell count by the calculated cell count: DCC / CCC 5,000,000 cells / 587,500 cells/mL = 8.5 mL = 8.5mL of the master culture contains 5 million cells. If the master culture was made up to a total of 100mL in step 11.1, it will be possible to aliquot a total of 11 samples for snap-freezing. 100mL (total volume) / 8.5 mL (required volume for DCC) = 11.76 = 11 complete samples of 5 million cells can be aliquoted from the master culture.

steps.title: Use a Styrofoam raft to snap-freeze...
steps.instruction: Use a Styrofoam raft to snap-freeze the tubes on a shallow bath of liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Remove the samples from the raft...
steps.instruction: Remove the samples from the raft and place into a sample box.

steps.title: Centrifuge all 1
steps.instruction: Centrifuge all 1.5mL tubes in a micro-centrifuge at the following specifications: A B 1 RCF Maximum RCF 2 TIME 10 minutes 3 TEMP 23 degrees celsius Figure 16. Centrifuge specifications for micro-centrifugation of 1.5mL tubes.

steps.title: Pour supernatant (seawater) into a liquid...
steps.instruction: Pour supernatant (seawater) into a liquid waste bin and carefully pipette any excess water from around the pellets. N.B seawater can affect the effectiveness of snap freezing. Figure 17. Drying the pellet and ensuring the removal of excess seawater prior to snap-freezing.

steps.title: and centrifuge in a benchtop centrifuge...
steps.instruction: and centrifuge in a benchtop centrifuge to the specifications outlined in Figure

steps.title: This example shows aliquots of two...
steps.instruction: This example shows aliquots of two desired cell count (DCC) amounts, left: 3 million cells and right: 5 million cells. A B 1 RCF 2500 x g 2 TIME 10 minutes 3 TEMP 23 degrees celsius Figure

steps.title: Centrifuge specifications for benchtop centrifuge for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge specifications for benchtop centrifuge for required volumes greater than 1.5mL.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet (pipette mix) in 1mL of seawater.

steps.title: For required volumes less than 1
steps.instruction: For required volumes less than 1.5mL , aliquot the required volume from the master culture directly into the 1.5mL LoBind Eppendorf tubes. N.B. Ensure the Symbiodiniaceae cells in the master culture are continually homogenised by pipette mixing.

steps.title: Pipette the resuspended Symbiodiniaceae cells into the labelled 1
steps.instruction: Pipette the resuspended Symbiodiniaceae cells into the labelled 1.5mL LoBind Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Label the necessary amount of 1
steps.instruction: Label the necessary amount of 1.5mL LoBind Eppendorf tubes with genotypic identification and quantity of cells (i.e Genotype M.aeq04, 3 million).

steps.title: Store in a -80C freezer until extraction
steps.instruction: Store in a -80C freezer until extraction.
